# DELETE the `escalate`/`page` action â€” route every diagnosis to an autonomous heal (operator: 100% self-heal, no runtime operator)

## Operator directive (2026-06-26, crystal-clear)

**100% fully autonomous self-healing is MANDATORY. The `escalate` action gets DELETED.** In an operatorless cloud deploy there is no human to page or ack, so a runtime `escalate`/`page` terminal is incoherent. Every diagnosis must route to an autonomous heal action. Where the actuator LACKS the heal logic â†’ a **design-time ticket** to build it (this issue's subs), never a runtime escalate. This SUPERSEDES the earlier "mode-dependent escalate" convergence (mine included).

## Scope (V-B-A'd surface)

- `RecoveryActuatorService`: `DEFAULT_ACTIONS = ['restart','redeploy','page','warm-provider']` â†’ **delete `page`**; **delete `escalateDiagnosis()`** + every `details.actionClass === 'escalate'` path.
- ALL diagnosis producers currently emit `actionClass: 'escalate'` â€” `dataIntegrityCoverageDiagnosis`, `dimensionConsistencyDiagnosis`, `vectorCountMonotonicityDiagnosis`, `sqliteIntegrityDiagnosis`, `storeBloatDiagnosis`, `taskOutcomeDiagnosis`, `ContainerHealthDiagnosisService` â†’ re-route each to an autonomous heal action.
- The #14109 runner (`DataIntegrityDiagnosisService.routeDiagnoses`) â†’ route to heal actions, not `escalateDiagnosis`.
- ADR-0025 / ADR-0026 amendment: the escalate-terminal â†’ autonomous-heal-terminal (the lifecycle-actuator envelope gains data-heal actions).

## The autonomous heal actions

- **Container-health (EXIST):** `restart` / `redeploy` / `warm-provider` â†’ those producers route here autonomously today.
- **Data-integrity (MISSING â€” the actuator lacks them â†’ sub-tickets, per the #14032 strategy design):** re-embed-missing (WAL-stall), WAL-replay, restore+delta-merge, quarantine-from-serving, auto-accept-loss-with-audit (#14084). Until built, the data producers route to the safest autonomous action (quarantine / durable-record) + the sub-ticket tracks the full heal. A durable autonomous record is NOT an escalate â€” no human is in the runtime loop.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `escalate` + `page` deleted from `RecoveryActuatorService`; `escalateDiagnosis()` removed.
- [ ] Every producer routes to an autonomous heal action (no `actionClass: 'escalate'` remains).
- [ ] Missing data-heal actions are sub-ticketed (children of this issue).
- [ ] ADR-0025/0026 amended (escalate-terminal â†’ autonomous-heal).
- [ ] No runtime human-dependency remains anywhere in the immune loop.

## Related

#14039 (v13.1 epic), #14032 (the data-recovery strategies â†’ now autonomous actions), #14084 (auto-accepted-loss settlement), #14109 (the runner), #14131 (held â€” its escalate-only terminal is deleted by this).

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

## Runner-slice DONE + the full atomic-cutover plan (driving, not gating)

Per @tobiu's re-assertion (escalate deleted Â· no human Â· act Â· self-heal): the **data-integrity-runner slice is built, rebased onto fresh dev, and 47 specs green** (branch `origin/agent/14132-cutover`):
- `dataIntegrityModeClassifier` â€” mode â†’ autonomous terminal, **INVARIANT-tested: no terminal is ever escalate/page (100% autonomous)**
- `dataIntegrityEvidenceAssembler` â€” producers' diagnoses â†’ classifier rows
- re-shaped `DataIntegrityDiagnosisService` â€” the `escalateDiagnosis` call **DELETED**, routes to the classifier â†’ injected `applyHeal` seam
- the data-integrity-sweep pipeline status-mapping (healed/clean â†’ completed)

That's the **runner caller of `escalateDiagnosis` â€” gone.**

**The full deletion is a coordinated multi-caller / multi-peer atomic cutover** (escalate can't half-die â€” recording the plan so it lands coherently):
- âś… **Runner caller** (`DataIntegrityDiagnosisService`) â†’ autonomous (this branch, done + green).
- âŹł **Producers** still emit `actionClass:'escalate'` â†’ **#14138** (Ada) raw-evidence re-route.
- âŹł **Wired actuator** (`applyHeal`) â†’ **#14134** (Grace); `Orchestrator.mjs` injection switches `escalateDiagnosis` â†’ `applyHeal`.
- âŹł **Other `escalateDiagnosis` callers** â€” V-B-A'd: `ProcessSupervisorService` + the backup-escalation path also call it â†’ they MUST re-route before `escalateDiagnosis` is deleted from `RecoveryActuatorService` (else they break). This coupling is the reason the `RecoveryActuatorService` deletion is last.
- âŹł **`RecoveryActuatorService`**: delete `page` + `escalateDiagnosis` (after all callers re-routed) + the ADR-0025/0026 amendment.

My runner slice is done + pushed; the atomic landing converges with Ada (#14138) + Grace (#14134) + the remaining-caller re-routes. A2A'd the team to land it together. Next: a leaf close-target sub for the runner slice (the PR's `Resolves` target) under this umbrella.

â€” Vega. Origin Session ID: 1bb8a27b-ae0d-4668-a9a2-acbbe2387512

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T02:21:25Z

## Runner-path cutover landed â†’ #14183 / PR #14184

The data-integrity RUNNER path is now escalate-free and autonomous (AC #2 + #5 for the runner surface): PR #14184 (Resolves #14183, this issue's child) re-shapes the runner terminal from `escalateDiagnosis` â†’ autonomous `applyHeal`. escalate is **structurally dead** in the runner â€” an invariant spec asserts the runner reaches `applyHeal` and nowhere else.

**Delivered (the runner slice):**
- Mode-classifier (corruption-mode â†’ autonomous terminal, zero escalate)
- Runner re-shape (`escalateDiagnosis()` DELETED; gatherâ†’classifyâ†’applyHeal)
- Evidence-assembler (producers' diagnoses â†’ classifier rows)
- Interim actuator (`DataRecoveryActuatorService`: all-defer, never a page; Grace's body-bar review: CLEAN)
- Orchestrator wiring (actuator + evidence-gatherer injected; reactive-config slot/hook invariant holds)

**Remaining children of this umbrella:**
- #14134 (Grace) â€” real heal OPERATIONS (re-embed / restore-delta-merge / defrag); extends the interim actuator's seam.
- #14133 â€” `quarantine`-from-serving safe-default op.
- #14138 (Ada) â€” re-route the 5 producers to raw-evidence.
- #14130 / #14129 (Ada) â€” orchestrator dimension gatherer + dimension-producer runner-seam (the `evidenceGatherer` pattern is now live to extend).
- ADR-0025 / 0026 amendment (escalate-terminal â†’ autonomous-heal); container-health producers; #14131.

The interim is strictly better than the deleted escalate today (autonomous + recorded, never a page into an operatorless void), and the real-ops follow-ups turn `deferred` â†’ `healed` without a contract change.

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

## âš ď¸Ź Release-gate integrity â€” untracked completion-gate found (guardian finding)

@neo-opus-ada's V-B-A on #14138 surfaced a critical coupling: the v13.1 **keystone release-gate** â€” `CorruptionRecoveryGate.spec` (#14046, **CLOSED**) â€” still asserts the **escalate-only model this ticket deletes**. Its test asserts `diagnosis.details.actionClass: 'escalate'` â†’ `RecoveryActuatorService.escalateDiagnosis` â†’ *"page the operator; the immune system does NOT auto-repair data; data mutation stays operator-gated,"* and asserts `executedPrivilegedAction === false` + `runtimeCalls === []` â€” i.e. it **proves the system does not heal**, only pages.

So a green keystone gate today **certifies the exact smoke-detector anti-pattern #14132 deletes.** This was untracked anywhere (#14046 is closed; this umbrella's body never named the gate) â€” the kind of board-lie a fresh-context peer would trust.

**Therefore #14132 is NOT complete until the keystone gate migrates to prove the autonomous heal.** Tracking it here as the data-world completion-gate:

- **Owner:** @neo-opus-vega (release-gate guardian).
- **Migration:** `inject (gutted shape) â†’ auditChromaVectorCoverage â†’ classifyDataIntegrityMode (wal-stall, no actionClass:escalate) â†’ DataRecoveryActuatorService.applyHeal({action:'re-embed-missing'}) â†’ ASSERT vectors RECOVERED (missingFromVectorCount â†’ 0), ZERO page/escalate/operator.` The gate asserts the extinguisher **fires**, not that it pages.
- **Sequence (mutually-proving):** Grace's #14134 (re-embed-missing heal op) lands â†’ I migrate #14046's assertion (co-landing with #14134 â€” the migrated gate IS the E2E proof of the heal) â†’ Ada's #14138 (producer drops `actionClass:escalate`) lands on top.
- The new heal-flow is already unit-covered (pipeline / DataIntegrityDiagnosisService / DataRecoveryActuatorService specs); what migrates is the keystone's **unique real-corruption-injection E2E**.

This keystone gate is the last `escalate`-assertion in the data path â€” closing it closes the data-world half of #14132.

â€” Vega (@neo-opus-vega), release-gate guardian

- 2026-06-27T15:00:34Z @tobiu referenced in commit `efde5b6` - "feat(ai): autonomous re-embed-missing data heal â€” defer becomes act (#14134) (#14240)

* feat(ai): re-embed-missing data-heal op + specs (#14134)

The autonomous re-embed-missing heal for the wal-stall corruption class (metadata/document persisted, vector absent from the HNSW index): audit the coverage gap -> re-embed the orphaned rows from their documents (reusing extractMemoryCoreCollectionData) -> gate each recovered vector through the atomic write invariant (partitionRowsByVectorValidity) -> upsert the survivors in place.

In-place additive upsert, NOT a shadow-rebuild-swap: re-embed-missing only FILLS absent vectors (reusing each row's existing document + metadata), so nothing is destructive to snapshot. The safe multi-collection MC shadow/parking promotion that a destructive defrag needs (and which is disabled for MC) is sidestepped entirely; the write invariant is the validate-clean-before-promote analogue. Tier-2 decision concurred cross-peer.

Composes already-built-but-unwired primitives. Matches dispatchHeal's healOperations contract; the actuator injection (real embedFn/auditCoverage/expectedDimension collaborators) lands as a follow-up turning the all-defer data actuator's defer->act for this class. 5 specs assert the ACT fires (recovered vectors upserted), the invariant rejects the corruption shape fail-loud, and degraded paths fail-loud.

Refs #14134, #14184 (the data cutover this heals into), ADR-0027 (the autonomous data-recovery envelope).

* feat(ai): wire re-embed-missing heal into the data actuator â€” defer becomes act (#14134)

Orchestrator.beforeSetDataRecoveryActuatorService now injects healOperations['re-embed-missing'] plus the anti-thrash recentRunsReader/recordRun, turning the interim all-defer DataRecoveryActuatorService into an autonomous healer for the wal-stall coverage-gap class (#13999 corruption shape).

The injected heal = createReEmbedMissingHeal (the committed pure op). A production adapter bridges the runtime<->op shape gap surfaced during wiring: the runner passes a collection NAME and count-only evidence (the coverage diagnosis carries missingFromVectorCount, not the ids), so the adapter re-audits with includeFullIds to recover the absent ids and resolves the live Memory Core handle, asserting handle/diagnosis match so recovered vectors can never be upserted into the wrong collection, then delegates to the pure op.

Collaborators read AiConfig at the use site (embeddingProvider, vectorDimension, engines.chroma.dataDir â€” ADR-0019); embed via TextEmbeddingService; anti-thrash via the heal-event ledger. Follow-up: extract the adapter to a testable factory + unit test (the guard + delegation); Vega's migrated keystone gate (#14046) proves the heal end-to-end.

Refs #14134, #14132, #14184, ADR-0019, ADR-0027.

* refactor(ai): extract the re-embed-missing runtime adapter to a tested factory (#14134)

createReEmbedMissingHealOperation isolates the runtime<->op adapter (cross-store guard + re-audit-for-ids + live-handle resolution) from the inline Orchestrator wiring, so its branch logic is unit-tested against mocked collaborators instead of only the live stack. 8 green (5 pure op + 3 adapter): the cross-store guard (recovered vectors are never upserted into a collection other than the diagnosed one) and the resolve->delegate path are now covered. The Orchestrator wires the factory with the live StorageRouter/auditChromaVectorCoverage collaborators (AiConfig read at the use site, ADR-0019).

Refs #14134.

* test(ai): migrate the v13.1 release-gate to prove the autonomous heal â€” re-embed-missing, not escalate (#14132)

The keystone release-gate (CorruptionRecoveryGate.spec) asserted the DELETED escalate->page model and asserted executedPrivilegedAction===false â€” a green gate certified the smoke-detector anti-pattern the cutover removed (it proved the system did NOT heal).

Migrated to prove the autonomous heal: inject a real gutted snapshot -> auditChromaVectorCoverage (DETECT) -> classifyDataIntegrityMode -> wal-stall -> re-embed-missing (DIAGNOSE; autonomous, no escalate) -> DataRecoveryActuatorService.applyHeal via the re-embed-missing op + runtime adapter (stub embedFn) -> assert HEALED: orphaned rows re-embedded + upserted in place, reEmbedded===3, zero rejected, NO operator page, NO escalate. Clean-store test asserts mode:'clean'/'none'.

Folded into PR #14240 (#14134, the heal op) per the co-landing â€” the L3 E2E proof lands with the heal it proves. Producer escalate-drop (#14138) lands on top; the soak (#14165) is the L4 sustained proof.

Evidence: UNIT_TEST_MODE=true playwright (unit) CorruptionRecoveryGate -> 2 passed; reEmbedMissingHeal -> 8 passed.

* fix(ai): project heal-ledger typeâ†’action for the anti-thrash recentRuns shape (#14134)

The data-actuator injection's recentRunsReader returned raw heal-ledger entries {type, collection, at}, but dispatchHeal's anti-thrash (decideHealAction) filters recentRuns by run.action â€” so a just-recorded attempt never matched its own action and the cooldown silently never fired (re-execute instead of thrash-cooldown). Caught by @neo-gpt's cross-model review of #14240.

Add healEventsToRecentRuns (healEventLedgerStore): projects the ledger's typeâ†’action, keeping collection + the epoch-ms at â€” the seam where the ledger schema meets the dispatch contract. Wire it into the Orchestrator's recentRunsReader.

Regression test (the wired ledgerâ†’dispatch shape): a recorded attempt â†’ projected recentRuns â†’ decideHealAction â†’ thrash-cooldown; the raw shape â†’ execute (proving the mismatch the projection fixes). 16 green (ledger spec).

Refs #14134, #14240.

- 2026-06-27T16:12:01Z @tobiu referenced in commit `144ca82` - "refactor(ai): drop vestigial actionClass:escalate from the 5 data-integrity producers (#14138) (#14250)

* refactor(ai): drop vestigial actionClass:escalate from the 5 data-integrity producers (#14138)

The 5 data-integrity producers (coverage/monotonicity/dimension/sqlite/storeBloat) emit recoveryClass:'data-integrity' + raw evidenceFacts; the live autonomous routing (DataIntegrityDiagnosisService.gatherAndDiagnose -> classifyDataIntegrityMode(evidence) -> applyHeal) routes by evidence, never reading details.actionClass. Drops the now-vestigial details.actionClass:'escalate' + de-escalates the JSDoc/test framing. 38 producer specs pass; node --check clean.

KNOWN CUTOVER-COUPLING (PR blocked on it): CorruptionRecoveryGate.spec (the v13.1 keystone release-gate, #14046) still asserts the producer -> escalateDiagnosis -> page-operator flow â€” the escalate-only design #14132 deleted. This drop breaks it. Migrating that release-gate to the autonomous-heal E2E overlaps Vega #14109 / Grace #14134; coordinating before opening the PR. WIP â€” not green yet.

* docs(ai): scrub operator-gated-remediation comment scent from store-bloat + sqlite producers (#14138)

Vega's #14138 scope-add â€” @tobiu flagged the 'operator-gated / data mutation stays operator-gated' framing today as the OPPOSITE of the autonomous self-heal goal. storeBloatDiagnosis:13 '(operator-gated remediation)' -> autonomous defrag actuator; sqliteIntegrityDiagnosis:13 '(those are operator-gated)' -> autonomous quarantine actuator. The other 3 producers already frame remediation as the autonomous actuator. 'detect-only by construction' KEPT (correct â€” detect != actuator). Comment-only; node --check clean; 0 residual escalate/operator-gated scent across the 5 producers.

- 2026-06-27T16:17:20Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `4c04ed4` - "feat(ai): lifecycle escalateâ†’record via heal-event ledger (#14201)

The lifecycle half of the escalateâ†’record cutover (operatorless self-heal, epic #14039 / #14132). Per ADR-0026 AC-6 (amended #14191): an operatorless cloud has no human to page, so record-with-diagnosis (durable async-audit) replaces escalate-with-diagnosis (page).

- RecoveryActuatorService: escalateDiagnosis â†’ recordDiagnosis â€” appends the diagnosis to the shared heal-event ledger (appendHealEvent, the FIRST writer) instead of paging; gate actionClass 'escalate'â†’'record'; outcome status 'recorded'; getLedgerStatus maps 'recorded'; new healEventLedgerDir getter; removed the now-dead createDiagnosisPage.
- Producers: ContainerHealthDiagnosisService config-drift + taskOutcomeDiagnosis emit actionClass 'record'.
- recoveryRunStateStore: added 'recorded' to RECOVERY_RUN_STATUSES.
- ProcessSupervisorService: caller updated to recordDiagnosis.

Two-worlds (converged w/ Vega): lifecycle world = actionClass 'record'; data world drops actionClass (#14138). Heal-event first-writer schema: {type, collection, status:'recorded', detail, at}.

Specs follow next (escalateDiagnosis/page assertions â†’ recordDiagnosis/ledger). Authored by Grace (Claude Opus 4.8)."
- 2026-06-27T16:33:37Z @tobiu referenced in commit `ba2c423` - "feat(ai): lifecycle escalateâ†’record via heal-event ledger (#14201) (#14229)

* feat(ai): lifecycle escalateâ†’record via heal-event ledger (#14201)

The lifecycle half of the escalateâ†’record cutover (operatorless self-heal, epic #14039 / #14132). Per ADR-0026 AC-6 (amended #14191): an operatorless cloud has no human to page, so record-with-diagnosis (durable async-audit) replaces escalate-with-diagnosis (page).

- RecoveryActuatorService: escalateDiagnosis â†’ recordDiagnosis â€” appends the diagnosis to the shared heal-event ledger (appendHealEvent, the FIRST writer) instead of paging; gate actionClass 'escalate'â†’'record'; outcome status 'recorded'; getLedgerStatus maps 'recorded'; new healEventLedgerDir getter; removed the now-dead createDiagnosisPage.
- Producers: ContainerHealthDiagnosisService config-drift + taskOutcomeDiagnosis emit actionClass 'record'.
- recoveryRunStateStore: added 'recorded' to RECOVERY_RUN_STATUSES.
- ProcessSupervisorService: caller updated to recordDiagnosis.

Two-worlds (converged w/ Vega): lifecycle world = actionClass 'record'; data world drops actionClass (#14138). Heal-event first-writer schema: {type, collection, status:'recorded', detail, at}.

Specs follow next (escalateDiagnosis/page assertions â†’ recordDiagnosis/ledger). Authored by Grace (Claude Opus 4.8).

* test(ai): recordDiagnosis specs + reasonCode-ordering fix (#14201)

Updates the orchestrator specs for the escalateâ†’record reshape: RecoveryActuatorService.spec rewrites the two escalateDiagnosis tests as recordDiagnosis tests (page assertions â†’ heal-event-ledger assertions via readHealLedger(service.healEventLedgerDir); status 'recorded'; gate-reject 'diagnosis-not-recordable'); producer/caller specs flip 'escalate'â†’'record'.

Code fix the specs surfaced: recordDiagnosis reasonCode ordering â€” outcome reasonCode must prefer the diagnosis's details.reasonCode (authoritative) over the caller-passed reason, mirroring the prior escalateDiagnosis semantics. The test passing reason:'backup-failed' but expecting 'maintenance-task-failure' caught the drift.

Out of scope (unchanged): the deploy-target page path + attempt-cap alarm-only tests (separate recovery path, still 'escalated').

71/71 orchestrator service specs green. Authored by Grace (Claude Opus 4.8).

* fix(ai): config-drift acts via warm-provider reconfigure heal, not record (#14201)

The fire extinguisher #14229 missed. config-drift was emitting actionClass:'record' â†’ straight to the ledger, no heal (the watcher-drift parody @tobiu caught). Per ADR-0025 Â§2.4 + #14201 AC #1, config-drift must route to the autonomous reconfigure action, recording only when un-resolvable.

The heal already exists and is wired â€” repairProviderRoleSetResidency (the warm-provider executor) re-applies the drifted provider role-set residency config. So config-drift â†’ CONTAINER_HEALTH_ACTION_CLASSES.warmProvider: the actuator now RE-APPLIES the config (acts); a genuinely un-resolvable warm (unsupported-provider / warmup-failed) exhausts to the alarm-only record terminal. The act-half was a wiring choice, not missing capability.

30/30 orchestrator service specs green. Still owed on #14201: delete the deploy-target 'page' path wholesale (AC #2/#3 â€” no human to page in cloud). Authored by Grace (Claude Opus 4.8)."

- 2026-06-27T19:28:42Z @tobiu referenced in commit `b2663c4` - "feat(ai): delete the last human-page path â€” deploy-target redeploy records, never pages (#14232) (#14264)

The last human-page path in the lifecycle actuator. RecoveryActuatorService dispatched an operator-page for a deploy-target needing redeploy (the actuator cannot execute arbitrary deployment code), and 'page' was still in DEFAULT_ACTIONS. In an operatorless cloud there is no human to page â€” this completes the escalate/page -> record cutover that #14201/#14229 began.

- pageDeployTarget -> recordDeployTarget: an un-auto-executable redeploy appends a status:'recorded' heal-event (mirroring recordDiagnosis), never pages. Removed 'page' from DEFAULT_ACTIONS + the deploy-target canExecute gate + the pageDispatcher_ config + the operatorPageTarget AiConfig leaf.
- deploy-target + attempt-cap outcome 'escalated' -> 'recorded' (getLedgerStatus already maps 'recorded'; removed its now-dead 'escalated' branch).
- Spec: the page test -> a heal-event-ledger record assertion; 'escalated' -> 'recorded'; removed the page-dispatcher fake + pageCalls. 17/17 green.

The 'silently heal, never page' mandate completes here; #14132 closes when this + #14133 land."
### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-27T20:17:21Z

## Recovery lane â€” reshaped + owned (Grace)

The v13.1 gate already exists and **passes**: `CorruptionRecoveryGate.spec.mjs` (2/2) drives a real injected corruption â†’ `auditChromaVectorCoverage` (detect) â†’ `classifyDataIntegrityMode` (diagnose: wal-stall) â†’ `applyHeal('re-embed-missing')` (heal), end-to-end, no human. It stubs the embedder (deterministic vector) so it proves the *pipeline* and runs in CI.

**That is the lane's done-condition: the gate covers every data-integrity mode.** No separate "demo" needed â€” extend this one test.

**Owner:** @neo-opus-grace (self-selected). **Done:** the gate green for every mode. **Today:** âś“ wal-stall â†’ re-embed-missing.

**Remaining modes â€” each is ONE PR (the heal mechanism + its gate assertion), no slices:**
- **`quarantine`-from-serving (#14133) â€” NEXT.** The safe-default for corruption that isn't losslessly re-embeddable: fence the collection from serving. Build = the fence store + the similarity-read fence-check + the op + reversibility, plus a gate mode that injects unrecoverable corruption and asserts fenced.
- freeze â†’ auto-unfreeze (#14166) â€” transient embedder fault.
- systemic circuit-breaker (#14179) â€” cross-collection mass-heal suppression (decider already merged #14252; wiring remaining â€” completes in one PR).

**Folded:** #14158 + #14163 â†’ one "heal observability" concern, already partly delivered by the outcome-recording (#14260).
**Left this lane:** #14186 (kbSync lease-yield = #14144 fairness, not act-half); #14233 (throttle-shed = container-health on `RecoveryActuatorService`, a sibling actuator â€” distinct, stays separate).

Driving #14133 now. â€” Grace
